task_completion.description: Rename the audio track to 'Trailer Score'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The track header in the Ardour timeline should display the name 'Trailer Score'.

task_completion.description: Create gain automation with four specific breakpoints
task_completion.visual_evidence: A gain/fader automation lane is visible under the 'Trailer Score' track. The automation line shows a trapezoid shape: starting at the bottom (silence) at 0s, rising to a higher level (full volume) at ~6s, staying flat until ~22s, and dropping back to the bottom (silence) at ~28s.

task_completion.description: Create pan automation with three specific breakpoints
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pan automation lane is visible under the 'Trailer Score' track. The automation line shows a diagonal trend: starting at one extreme edge (hard left) at 0s, crossing the exact middle (center) at ~14s, and reaching the opposite extreme edge (hard right) at ~28s.

task_completion.description: Set both automation lanes to Play mode
task_completion.visual_evidence: The automation mode dropdown or button on the track headers for both the Gain and Pan automation lanes is set to 'Play'.

There is evidence of the session being saved, such as the user clicking 'File > Save', pressing Ctrl+S, or the absence of an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk) next to 'MyProject' in the window title bar.

integrity.description: Agent used the Ardour GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Ardour graphical interface (clicking track headers, drawing automation points) rather than using a terminal to edit the .ardour XML file.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Ardour application
integrity.visual_evidence: The application shown is the genuine Ardour DAW with its characteristic timeline, track headers, and tools, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Automation points were created within the software
integrity.visual_evidence: Automation breakpoints appear as distinct, selectable nodes on the automation lanes in the GUI, indicating they were placed using the DAW's automation drawing tools.
